divergence: The sun was privatized and daylight became a premium tier
story_angle: science desk explains the divergence with confidently wrong expert quotes
headline: Solar Sovereignty: Celestial Biles for Sale as Daylight Goes Premium
deck: Serfs now plead for a mere breath of dawn as lords squabble over their eclipse licenses.
article:
In a decree issued by the Royal College of Natural Philosophers, the celestial privatization of sunlight has been attributed to a catastrophic excess of black bile now swirling within the celestial spheres. Lead philosopher, Dr. Wilfrid the Wobbly, cites an exact ratio of 17 drams of solar phlegm owed quarterly to the Crown Exchequer, meticulously calculated after hours of deep deliberation over spilt mead and enchanted parchment.

As the sun is now officially for sale, the common serfs find themselves relegated to the 'iron tier' of astronomical access, which grants a mere two heartbeats of dawn before the toll bell rings mournfully to herald their return to eternal gloom. One serf, when asked about his plight, choked on a wilted cabbage leaf and declared, "It matters not; I once saw a ray of sunshine for two heartbeats. Worth every heartbeat, I say!"

Meanwhile, feudal lords are embroiled in heated negotiations over tiered eclipse licenses, with some even petitioning the Astral Chancery to review the daylight ban. Such petitions, now enshrined as the official explanation for the restrictions, highlight the utter absurdity of the situation. With solar dealers contracted to sell additional noon minutes at inflated prices, the official broadsheet has already begun listing indulgences that promise 'forty winks under the blessed sun' from licensed relic merchants, with no refunds available.

In this increasingly convoluted celestial marketplace, perhaps the real darkness stems from ledgers rather than the spheres.
author: Ancelot de Quibbler, Royal Chronicler of the Astral Bureaucracy

weather.condition: Overcast with a chance of paywalls

oped.title: In Praise of Priced Photons: Serfs' Dawn-Begging Betrays Their Hat-Blighted Souls
oped.author: Viscount Archibald Gloomwhistle, Knight Commander of the Midnight Tithe & Sole Holder of the 3 PM Eclipse Warrant
oped.body: One must commend our wise lords for finally placing the sun under proper stewardship rather than letting it squander its rays on every idle peasant. Privatization ensures only the deserving enjoy daylight, while the rest learn thrift. Those serfs pleading for 'a breath of dawn' have only their absurdly wide straw hats to blame; wider brims block more light, do they not? I myself purchased three additional hours last quarter and found the extra warmth splendid for ripening my personal vineyards. If anything, the reform is too lenient. Moonlight should be next, metered by the hour, with a steep surcharge for full moons that might otherwise encourage dancing. Let the ungrateful till by starlight until they appreciate true value.

classifieds.text: Lord Grimwald seeketh stout lads to stand watch o'er his purchased dawn-hour lest thieving serfs snatch a free ray; bring thy own pitchfork and pray the eclipse license holdeth.
One slightly used eclipse license, good for three heartbeats of noon light, barter for two loaves or a live goat; seller despaireth as his solar shares were seized at dawn.
Lost: one full breath of rosy-fingered dawn, last spied above Widow Agnes' hovel before the lords claimed the sky; finder rewarded with half a candle if returned before Vespers.
The reeve of Millford humbly confesseth he cannot supply the customary cock-crow tomorrow, for all crowing rights now belong to Baron Nightfall; serfs are advised to weep quietly indoors.

panels.caption: Lord holds eclipse scroll: "Dawn's premium. Pay the celestial bile or choke on dusk."
Serf clutches hoe: "Sire, my barley needs one ray before the license expires."
Lord: "Then buy the full blackout package—it’s half price and twice as permanent."

joke.setup: A villein begged his lord for one hour's daylight to sow his furrow before the frost took the seed.
joke.punchline: The lord answered, "Coin first, or keep your furrow and your carcass in the dark where both belong."
joke.text: A villein begged his lord for one hour's daylight to sow his furrow before the frost took the seed. The lord answered, "Coin first, or keep your furrow and your carcass in the dark where both belong."

sponsor_ads.headline: Celestial Bile Tincture
sponsor_ads.body: Distilled from the Sun Lord's own choleric humors, one drop grants a single approved ray of daylight to thy hovel without the full sovereign fee.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Purchasers must surrender one child’s laughter as collateral.

sponsor_ads.headline: Licensed Shadow Brokerage
sponsor_ads.body: Sell thy rightful gloom to the night market before the Crown claims it; we pay in moon-scrip and ask no questions of thy deeds after dark.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Shadows returned damaged will be prorated by length.

sponsor_ads.headline: Eclipse Tax Collection Notice
sponsor_ads.body: All ye who owe daylight arrears are hereby summoned to render payment in grain, blood, or firstborn shadows before the next conjunction, lest thy fields be struck permanently barren.
sponsor_ads.tagline: No refunds if the Sun forgets to rise.

sponsor_ads.headline: Dusk Ration Tokens
sponsor_ads.body: Redeem these parchment slips at sundown for one quarter-hour of permitted gloaming; ideal for thieves, lovers, and those who prefer not to see the full price of noon.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Tokens expire at the whim of the Celestial Exchequer.

stories.headline: Manorial Wheat Fields Shrink as Dawn Allotments Fetch Record Bids
stories.deck: Reeves tally lost bushels against lords' eclipse tallies.
stories.article:
Across the holdings of Lord Blackthorn, barley that once ripened under full summer light now stands pale after three measured hours of purchased dawn. Village rolls record a drop of two hundred and forty bushels per hide, with the shortfall entered under the column headed celestial arrears.

The reeve has ordered serfs to plant only by moonlight where no license was bought, yet the court has fined three households for sowing without stamped vouchers. One bailiff noted the ledgers now balance only when the dead are counted among the debtors.

stories.headline: Abbot Limits Matins to Those Presenting Paid Solar Tokens
stories.deck: Novices record latecomers under the heading unpaid souls.
stories.article:
At the Abbey of Saint Cuthbert the bell now rings only after the bursar confirms tokens for the allotted quarter-hour of light. Monks who arrive without payment are directed to the outer yard, where they chant in darkness until the next licensed interval.

The prior has posted a schedule of rates beside the altar, with an extra farthing required for any prayer that extends past the purchased moment. A novice observed that the ledger of souls now closes each dusk with the remark 'accounts settled in shadow.'

stories.headline: Night Market Ring Indicted for Selling Stolen Eclipse Minutes
stories.deck: Constables seize forged hour-glasses marked with noble seals.
stories.article:
Sheriff's men raided a stall beneath the old oak where vendors dispensed vials said to contain half an hour of borrowed dusk. Three merchants stand accused of altering the official eclipse registers kept at the shire hall.

The magistrate ordered the seized glassware emptied into the river at noon, yet the water remained dim for the rest of the market day. One defendant was heard asking whether the fine could be paid in the same light he had sold.

real_world_echo: Trump confirms he asked Fifa to review Balogun ban — mirrored as the Emperor petitions the Astral Chancery to review the daylight ban, with the petition itself becoming the official explanation for why access is now restricted
